title: Refactor install into add + update
1shots
body: Small, single-session work items.

title: Add pre-commit hook for vendor file protection
body:
Add a pre-commit hook that enforces the same vendor file protection as the check-vendor GHA workflow, vendored to consumer repos via install.sh.

## Design

- Add `--staged` flag to `vendored/check` — checks `git diff --cached --name-only` (staged files) instead of branch diff
- Create `vendored/hooks/pre-commit` — thin shell script calling `python3 .vendored/check --staged`
- Add `--install-hook` flag to `vendored/check` — symlinks `.vendored/hooks/pre-commit` into `.git/hooks/pre-commit`, force-overwriting any existing hook (including pearls)
- Update `install.sh` to download the hook to `.vendored/hooks/pre-commit`

## Acceptance criteria

### `--staged` mode
- [ ] `vendored/check --staged` checks staged files via `git diff --cached --name-only`
- [ ] Reuses all existing protection logic (patterns, allowed lists, install-branch skip)
- [ ] Exits 0 if no staged violations, 1 if violations found
- [ ] Works both in CI and locally (no dependency on GitHub env vars)

### `vendored/hooks/pre-commit`
- [ ] Shell script at `vendored/hooks/pre-commit` that runs `python3 .vendored/check --staged`
- [ ] Exits with the check script exit code
- [ ] Executable permissions set

### `--install-hook` mode
- [ ] `vendored/check --install-hook` symlinks `.git/hooks/pre-commit` to `.vendored/hooks/pre-commit` (relative: `../../.vendored/hooks/pre-commit`)
- [ ] Force-overwrites any existing pre-commit hook (file or symlink) — squashes pearls etc.
- [ ] Verifies `.vendored/hooks/pre-commit` exists before symlinking, errors if missing
- [ ] Prints confirmation message on success

### install.sh
- [ ] Downloads `vendored/hooks/pre-commit` to `.vendored/hooks/pre-commit`
- [ ] Sets executable permissions (`chmod +x`)
- [ ] Creates `.vendored/hooks/` directory

### Tests
- [ ] Unit tests for `--staged` mode in `tests/test_check.py` (staged files checked, protection logic applied, violations reported)
- [ ] Unit test for `--install-hook` (symlink created, overwrites existing, errors on missing hook file)

### Docs
- [ ] Update `install.sh` header comment to document the new hook file
- [ ] Add `--staged` and `--install-hook` to the check script docstring

title: Vendor contract v2: manifest-driven install/check/remove
body: Redesign the vendor contract around a manifest-based approach. Flatten repo structure, merge add+update into install, add remove command, and shift version/metadata ownership to the framework.

title: Directory Restructure: Consolidate Vendor Files Under .vendored/
body:
Consolidate per-vendor dotdirs (`.pearls/`, `.dogfood/`, `.semver/`) and the shared `config.json` into a structured layout under `.vendored/`:

- **`configs/<vendor>.json`** — per-vendor config (replaces monolithic `config.json`)
- **`pkg/<vendor>/`** — vendor-installed files (replaces per-vendor dotdirs at repo root)

This eliminates dotdir sprawl and merge conflicts from concurrent vendor update PRs.

**Depends on:** gv-cf3f (v2 contract — manifests, env vars) — all tasks closed.

title: Unify vendor registry and project config under _vendor key
body:
After gv-a34c lands, per-vendor configs live at `.vendored/configs/<vendor>.json` with registry fields at the top level. This epic introduces a `_vendor` namespace key to separate framework-managed registry fields from vendor-defined project config, then merges existing per-vendor project configs into the same files.

**Target schema:**
```json
{
  "_vendor": {
    "repo": "mangimangi/pearls",
    "install_branch": "chore/install-pearls",
    "automerge": true,
    "protected": [".vendored/pkg/pearls/**"],
    "allowed": [".vendored/configs/pearls.json"]
  },
  "prefix": "gv",
  "docs": ["AGENTS.md"],
  "models": { "implementer": "claude-opus-4-6" }
}
```

- `_vendor` — framework-owned, written by install/remove, read by check. Underscore signals "managed."
- Top-level keys — project-owned, opaque to framework, read by the vendor tool itself.

**Depends on:** gv-a34c (directory restructure — per-vendor configs and pkg/ layout).

title: Vendor Contract Validation GHA
body:
Expose vendor contract checks as a standalone validation tool and GitHub Action workflow. Allows answering "does repo XYZ validly implement the git-vendored vendor contract?" without actually installing the vendor.

See docs/planning/epics/gv-9d4e-validation-gha.md for full design.

title: Vendor dependency management
body:
Extend the vendor contract to support vendor-to-vendor dependencies. Vendors declare which other vendors they require via an optional deps.json at repo root, and the framework checks (and optionally installs) those dependencies during the install flow.

## Motivation

Today, if vendor A requires vendor B to function, there's no way to express or enforce this. The failure mode is silent: vendor A installs fine, then breaks at runtime because vendor B isn't present. Consumers have to debug missing deps themselves; vendor authors resort to README notes.

## Key Design Decisions

- **deps.json at repo root** (not emitted by install.sh) — deps must be checked before running install.sh
- **Match deps by repo field**, not vendor name — handles consumers who install with custom --name
- **Dependency modes**: error (default, safe), warn, install (auto-install), skip
- **Version constraints deferred** — v1 is presence-only
- **Topological sort for install all** — prevents ordering failures
- **Cache resolved deps** in .vendored/manifests/<vendor>.deps for reverse-dep lookups

## Planning Doc

docs/planning/dependency-management.md

title: Multi-vendor PR commit fails: pre-commit hook blocks chore/install-vendors branch
body:
## Problem

When multiple vendors update simultaneously (e.g., git-semver + git-vendored), `create_pull_request()` in `.vendored/install` fails with `CalledProcessError` on `git commit`.

**Root cause:** The multi-vendor branch name `chore/install-vendors` (line 636) does not match any individual vendor's `install_branch` prefix (e.g., `chore/install-git-vendored`, `chore/install-git-semver`). The pre-commit hook runs `.vendored/check --staged`, which checks branch matching at `check_vendor()` line 181 — since no prefix matches, it treats all staged vendor files as protection violations and exits 1.

Single-vendor updates work because their branch name (e.g., `chore/install-git-vendored-v0.0.11`) starts with the vendor's `install_branch`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `create_pull_request()` succeeds when multiple vendors update simultaneously
- [ ] The fix uses `git commit --no-verify` in `create_pull_request()` since the install script IS the authorized mechanism for modifying vendor files (the pre-commit hook guards against manual edits, not automated installs)
- [ ] Add a code comment explaining why `--no-verify` is used
- [ ] Add a test that verifies `create_pull_request` passes `--no-verify` to git commit
- [ ] Existing single-vendor PR creation still works

## Reproduction

Run `python3 .vendored/install all --pr` when 2+ vendors have updates available, with the pre-commit hook installed. The `git commit` step in `create_pull_request()` will fail with exit code 1.

## Files to Change

- `.vendored/install` line 681: add `--no-verify` flag to git commit
- `tests/` — add test coverage for multi-vendor commit with pre-commit hook

title: Use git push --force-with-lease for ephemeral install branches
body:
## Problem
`create_pull_request()` does a plain `git push -u origin <branch>`, which
fails as a non-fast-forward when the ephemeral install branch (e.g.
`chore/install-vendors`) already exists on the remote from a prior run.

## Fix
Change to `git push --force-with-lease -u origin <branch>`.

This is safe because these branches are ephemeral automation branches, not
shared work. `--force-with-lease` still guards against the edge case where
someone else pushed to the branch concurrently.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `templates/install` push command uses `--force-with-lease`
- [ ] `.vendored/install` push command uses `--force-with-lease`
- [ ] Brief inline comment explaining why force-with-lease is appropriate
- [ ] Test asserts `--force-with-lease` is present in the push command
- [ ] Existing tests still pass

title: Exclude workflow files from automated install PR commits
body:
## Problem
`create_pull_request()` runs `git add -A` which stages `.github/workflows/`
files written by install.sh. The `GITHUB_TOKEN` used in CI lacks `workflows`
permission, so `git push` is rejected with:

> refusing to allow a GitHub App to create or update workflow without
> `workflows` permission

## Root cause
install.sh always installs workflow templates (lines 112-113) and includes
them in the manifest. `git add -A` blindly stages everything. These workflow
files should only be installed during initial bootstrap, not re-committed in
automated update PRs.

## Fix
After `git add -A`, run `git reset HEAD -- .github/workflows/` to unstage
workflow files before committing. This keeps install.sh's behavior unchanged
(it still writes them to disk for initial installs) but excludes them from
the automated PR commit.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `create_pull_request()` unstages `.github/workflows/` after `git add -A`
- [ ] Both `templates/install` and `.vendored/install` are updated
- [ ] Test verifies `git reset HEAD -- .github/workflows/` is called
- [ ] Existing tests still pass

title: Self-bootstrap coordinator on git-vendored install
body:
## Problem
When any consumer repo updates git-vendored, the OLD `.vendored/install`
coordinator runs in memory while the NEW `install.sh` writes the fixed
coordinator to disk. Post-install steps (PR creation, output) execute
using the OLD in-memory code. If the OLD coordinator has a bug in those
post-install paths, the install fails, the fix never gets committed, and
the repo is stuck indefinitely.

Example: the force-push fix in `create_pull_request()` — install.sh wrote
the fixed coordinator to disk, but the old `create_pull_request()` was
still executing in memory.

## Root cause
`download_and_run_install()` runs the vendor's install.sh, which overwrites
`.vendored/install` on disk. But the Python process continues executing the
old version from memory. There is no mechanism to hand off to the new
coordinator for post-install steps.

## Solution
After `download_and_run_install()` returns, detect that the on-disk
`.vendored/install` has changed (hash comparison against `__file__`).
If changed, re-exec the process:

```
python3 .vendored/install git-vendored --pr --post-install-only
```

The `--post-install-only` flag tells the NEW coordinator to skip
`download_and_run_install()` and only run post-install steps (build
result from on-disk manifest/version state, create PR, write output).

### Flow
1. `python3 .vendored/install git-vendored --pr` (OLD coordinator)
2. OLD coordinator calls `download_and_run_install()` → new install.sh
   writes new coordinator to disk
3. OLD coordinator detects `.vendored/install` on disk differs from
   running script (hash comparison)
4. OLD coordinator re-execs:
   `python3 .vendored/install git-vendored --pr --post-install-only`
5. NEW coordinator sees `--post-install-only`, reads version/manifest
   from disk, creates PR

### Scope
This is specifically for git-vendored updating its own coordinator.
No other vendor's install.sh writes to `.vendored/install`, so no
generalization is needed. Detection should key off whether the on-disk
coordinator actually changed, not vendor name or dogfood flag.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Add `--post-install-only` flag to CLI argument parser
- [ ] After `download_and_run_install()`, hash-compare `__file__` against
      on-disk `.vendored/install`; if changed, re-exec with `--post-install-only`
      and forward all relevant flags (--pr, --version, --force)
- [ ] `--post-install-only` code path reads installed version and manifest
      from disk, builds result dict, runs PR creation and output
- [ ] Guard against infinite re-exec (e.g. if `--post-install-only` is set,
      never re-exec again)
- [ ] Add tests for the bootstrap detection and re-exec path
- [ ] Update docs if install CLI reference exists

title: Vendored Session Hooks — Framework-Level Hook Lifecycle
Revert direct edits to .vendored/install — changes must come through install workflow
Codex Support & Vendor DX Improvements
Implement $VENDOR_LIB shell helper library
body:
## Summary

Create `.vendored/lib/vendor-helpers.sh` — a shell library that vendor `install.sh` scripts can source to avoid duplicating download/auth/manifest logic.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.vendored/lib/vendor-helpers.sh` exists with:
  - `fetch_file <repo_path> <local_path> [+x]` — downloads from `$VENDOR_REPO` at `$VENDOR_REF`, optionally `chmod +x`, appends to `$VENDOR_MANIFEST`
  - `fetch_dir <repo_path> <local_path>` — downloads directory tree, appends all files to `$VENDOR_MANIFEST`
  - Auth handled via `$GH_TOKEN` / `$VENDOR_PAT`
- [ ] New env var `$VENDOR_LIB` passed to vendor `install.sh` by `.vendored/install`, pointing to `.vendored/lib/vendor-helpers.sh`
- [ ] Bootstrap `install.sh` creates the `lib/` dir and installs `vendor-helpers.sh`
- [ ] Existing vendor `install.sh` scripts continue to work unchanged (backwards compat)
- [ ] Tests covering: fetch_file with/without +x, fetch_dir, auth header, manifest emission, $VENDOR_LIB not set (graceful fallback)

## Vendor Repo Prereqs

No blocking prereqs. After this ships, vendor repos can optionally adopt the helpers:

### mangimangi/pearls

Update `install.sh` to source `$VENDOR_LIB` with inline fallback:

```bash
source "$VENDOR_LIB" 2>/dev/null || {
    fetch_file() { curl -fsSL ...; }  # existing logic
}

fetch_file "prl.py" "$VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR/prl.py"
fetch_file "merge-driver.py" "$VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR/merge-driver.py"
# etc.
```

### mangimangi/madreperla

Same pattern — source `$VENDOR_LIB` with fallback, replace inline curl calls with `fetch_file`.

title: Implement .vendored/feedback command and support config
body:
## Summary

Add a `support` config key to vendor config and a standalone `.vendored/feedback` script that surfaces bug-reporting info for installed vendors.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Vendor config schema supports optional `support` key:
  ```json
  {
    "support": {
      "issues": "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues",
      "instructions": "Include output of ...",
      "labels": ["bug", "feature-request"]
    }
  }
  ```
- [ ] Smart default: if `support` is absent, derive `issues` URL from `repo` field
- [ ] `.vendored/feedback` script (Python, framework-owned):
  - `python3 .vendored/feedback` — shows support info for all vendors
  - `python3 .vendored/feedback <vendor>` — shows support info for specific vendor
  - Human-readable output format (no --json for now)
- [ ] Bootstrap installs `.vendored/feedback` alongside install/check/remove
- [ ] Orchestrator (`vendored-session.sh`) emits a breadcrumb listing ALL vendors:
  > Installed tools with feedback loops: pearls, git-semver, git-vendored. Run \`.vendored/feedback <tool>\` for details.
- [ ] Tests covering: smart default derivation, explicit support config, feedback output, breadcrumb emission

## Vendor Repo Prereqs

No blocking prereqs. Vendors can optionally add `support` to their `_vendor` config for richer detail:

### mangimangi/pearls

Add to `_vendor` in config:
```json
"support": {
  "issues": "https://github.com/mangimangi/pearls/issues",
  "instructions": "Include the output of `prl list` and your .pearls/issues.jsonl in bug reports.",
  "labels": ["bug", "feature-request"]
}
```

### mangimangi/madreperla

Add to `_vendor` in config:
```json
"support": {
  "issues": "https://github.com/mangimangi/madreperla/issues",
  "instructions": "Include your .madreperla/config.json and the failing prompt output.",
  "labels": ["bug"]
}
```

Without these changes, `.vendored/feedback` still works — it falls back to deriving the issues URL from the `repo` field.

title: Implement --setup-hooks for multi-agent hook orchestration
body:
## Summary

New command `python3 .vendored/install --setup-hooks [agent]` that generates agent-specific config files pointing to a framework-owned orchestrator. Supports Claude Code and Codex. Moves hook orchestration ownership from madreperla to git-vendored.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--setup-hooks [agent]` on `.vendored/install`:
  - `agent` = `claude`, `codex`, or omitted (auto-detect based on which dotdir exists; no-op if neither)
  - For Claude: generates/updates `.claude/settings.json` with SessionStart/resume hooks pointing to `.vendored/hooks/vendored-session.sh`
  - For Codex: generates/updates `.codex/config.toml` with SessionStart hook (format per Codex v0.114.0+ experimental hooks)
  - Idempotent — safe to re-run after vendor install/remove
- [ ] Move orchestrator from `.claude/hooks/vendored-session.sh` to `.vendored/hooks/vendored-session.sh`
  - Replace `PROJECT_DIR` derivation: use `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` instead of directory traversal
  - Keep backward compat: if old path exists, remove it during migration
- [ ] Orchestrator is in git-vendored's own manifest (framework-owned file)
- [ ] Agent config files (`.claude/settings.json`, `.codex/config.toml`) are NOT in any manifest — they're project-owned, framework-generated
- [ ] Bootstrap `install.sh` runs `--setup-hooks` after initial install
- [ ] Tests covering: Claude setup, Codex setup, auto-detect, no-op when no agent dirs, idempotency, orchestrator path migration

## Vendor Repo Prereqs

### mangimangi/pearls (non-blocking, needed for Codex end-to-end)

Replace `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` fallback with `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` in 4 hook files:

**Files to change:**
- `.vendored/pkg/pearls/hooks/session-start.sh` (or source equivalent)
- `.vendored/pkg/pearls/hooks/session-resume.sh`
- `.vendored/pkg/pearls/hooks/post-install.sh`
- `.vendored/pkg/pearls/hooks/pre-push`

**Change in each file:**
```bash
# Before:
PROJECT_DIR="${PROJECT_DIR:-${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)}}"

# After:
PROJECT_DIR="${PROJECT_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)}"
```

One-line change per file. No functional change in practice — `$PROJECT_DIR` is already set by the orchestrator. But without this, hooks fail when invoked by Codex (which doesn't set `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`).

### mangimangi/madreperla (BLOCKING — must land before git-vendored takes over)

Delete all hook generation code. After this change, madreperla focuses purely on prompt content.

**Code to remove:**
- `--install-hooks` CLI flag and argument handling
- `write_settings_json()` function
- `_build_vendored_entries()` function
- `ORCHESTRATOR_SCRIPT` constant
- `VENDORED_MARKER` constant
- Remove "hi claude" from `get_prompt_intro()` (parameterize greeting or remove entirely)

**Keep:** All prompt generation logic (`build_prompt()`, `get_prompt_intro()` minus greeting, template rendering, etc.)

This is a net deletion — no new code needed in madreperla.

title: templates/install and .vendored/install diverge in setup_hooks behavior
Missing fetch_dir tests and VENDOR_PAT auth test in vendor-helpers
setup_hooks auto-detect ignores dotdir presence, always configures all agents
vendor-helpers _vendor_download fails in CI when real gh is on PATH
PR staging misses .vendored/pkg/ files when --post-install-only re-execs
body:
## Problem

The --pr codepath in .vendored/install creates PRs that are missing .vendored/pkg/<vendor>/ files. Manifests, configs, and hooks are committed, but the actual vendor code (prl.py, AGENTS.md, hook scripts, etc.) is not.

Discovered in medici PR #5 — GHA install of madreperla (with pearls as a dependency) produced a PR with 12 files, none from .vendored/pkg/.

## Root cause

When self-bootstrap triggers a re-exec with --post-install-only (line 1496-1517), the new process only builds a result for args.target (the main vendor). Dependency files are on disk (written by the initial install), and git add -A (line 980) stages them, but the --post-install-only path was designed for single-vendor re-execs, not dependency multi-vendor installs. The result metadata only reflects the main vendor.

Worth investigating:
1. Does git add -A actually stage pkg/ files, or is there a working directory issue?
2. Does the dependency install (pearls) complete before or after _maybe_reexec triggers?
3. The --post-install-only path should detect ALL changed vendors by scanning manifests, not just args.target.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] When --pr is used with --deps=install, the PR includes all .vendored/pkg/ files for both the main vendor and its dependencies
- [ ] The --post-install-only path aggregates results for all vendors that were installed (main + deps)
- [ ] Test: mock a vendor with a dependency, run install with --pr --deps=install, verify PR diff includes pkg/ files for both

title: --private flag doesn't propagate through dependency auto-install
body:
## Problem

When installing a private vendor with --deps=install, the coordinator auto-installs dependencies via resolve_deps → install_new_vendor. But the dependency's private status is unknown:

1. The dep has no per-vendor config yet (first-time install in the consumer repo)
2. The dep's entry in deps.json has no private field — it only contains repo and optionally version
3. install_new_vendor doesn't receive a private flag, so get_auth_token falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN instead of VENDOR_PAT

Even though the parent's token is passed through (which may be VENDOR_PAT), get_auth_token inside install_existing_vendor checks vendor_config.get("private") to decide whether VENDOR_PAT is required. For a new dep with no config, this check fails.

## Example

madreperla depends on pearls (private repo). In the consumer repo:

  VENDOR_PAT=<token> python3 .vendored/install mangimangi/madreperla --private --deps=install

The coordinator has auth for madreperla, but when it auto-installs pearls, there's no pearls.json config yet, so the coordinator doesn't know pearls needs VENDOR_PAT.

## Fix options

1. **Per-dep private declaration in deps.json**: Allow deps.json to include a private field per dependency:
   ```json
   {"pearls": {"repo": "mangimangi/pearls", "private": true}}
   ```
   resolve_deps passes this to install_new_vendor which uses it for get_auth_token.

2. **Inherit parent's --private flag**: If the parent is private, assume deps are too. Simple but incorrect when a private vendor depends on a public one (over-requires VENDOR_PAT).

3. **Both**: Per-dep declaration with parent flag as fallback.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] deps.json supports an optional private field per dependency
- [ ] resolve_deps passes the private flag to install_new_vendor when auto-installing
- [ ] install_new_vendor uses the private flag for get_auth_token on first-time dep installs
- [ ] Test: mock a private vendor with a private dependency, verify --deps=install works with only VENDOR_PAT set
- [ ] Docs: update vendor contract section to document the private field in deps.json

title: Missing docs: deps.json private field not documented in vendor contract

body: Schema handling, audit command, and registry migration for vendor config validation. All consumer repos are blocked on this work shipping first.

title: image-baked medici: remove session-hook subsystem from git-vendored
body: Phase 4.5 of mdci-cf3d. All target repos have migrated to image-baked model. Delete dead session-hook subsystem while preserving core vendoring for git-semver/git-dogfood. See medici docs/planning/epics/mdci-cf3d/git-vendored.md.

title: Config split: create pearls.yaml and madreperla.yaml, clean up medici.yaml
body:
Part of medici config split (mdci-23b1 phase 3).

Create pearls.yaml (prefix: gv, eval.threshold: 80) and madreperla.yaml (description, docs, providers) at repo root. Remove flat-field identity config from medici.yaml. Clean up .vendored/configs/pearls.json if it duplicates config. Verify prl and madp resolve config correctly.

Source planning doc: medici/docs/orchestration/config-split/git-vendored.md

title: Migrate to .pearls/docs/ and simplified providers config
body: Per-repo migration: move doc dirs to .pearls/docs/, simplify madreperla.yaml providers.

title: Migrate docs to .pearls/docs/ and simplify providers config
body:
Atomic migration: move planning docs to `.pearls/docs/`, simplify `madreperla.yaml` providers, and regenerate commands. Per the planning doc, this is one issue because partial states (e.g. moved docs but old providers config) would break command generation.

## Scope

### 1. Update pearls.yaml

Add top-level `docs:` section mapping each doc category to its `.pearls/docs/` path:

```yaml
docs:
  planning: .pearls/docs
  epics: .pearls/docs/epics
  ideas: .pearls/docs/ideas
  headaches: .pearls/docs/headaches
  retros: .pearls/docs/retros
```

### 2. Move doc directories

- `mkdir -p .pearls/docs/retros`
- `git mv docs/planning/epics .pearls/docs/epics` (skip if absent)
- `git mv docs/planning/ideas .pearls/docs/ideas` (skip if absent)
- `git mv docs/planning/headaches .pearls/docs/headaches` (skip if absent)
- Move any standalone `docs/planning/*.md` files to `.pearls/docs/` root (e.g. `gh-extension.md`)
- `touch .pearls/docs/retros/.gitkeep`
- `rmdir docs/planning` if empty

Use `2>/dev/null || true` on `git mv` / `rmdir` so missing dirs don't abort the migration.

### 3. Simplify madreperla.yaml

Replace the existing `providers:` block with:

```yaml
providers:
  issues: pearls
  docs: pearls
```

Leave `docs: [AGENTS.md, README.md, docs/]` (the repo context list) unchanged.

### 4. Regenerate

- `prl configure`
- `madp configure --provider claude`

### 5. Verify

- `grep -r '{docs\.' commands/` must find nothing (no unresolved `{docs.*}` literals in generated commands).

## Acceptance criteria

- `.pearls/docs/` contains `epics/`, `ideas/`, `headaches/`, `retros/` subdirs (retros has `.gitkeep`).
- No `docs/planning/epics/`, `docs/planning/ideas/`, `docs/planning/headaches/` directories remain.
- `docs/planning/` is empty or removed; any standalone `.md` files have moved to `.pearls/docs/`.
- `madreperla.yaml` `providers:` block is simplified to `{issues: pearls, docs: pearls}`.
- `pearls.yaml` has a `docs:` section pointing at `.pearls/docs/` paths.
- Generated commands under `commands/` have fully resolved paths (no `{docs.*}` literals).
- All changes committed and pushed in a single commit.

title: Git-semver files audit
body:
# Git-semver files audit

> Epic: `mdci-dev-2153` (orchestrator), per-repo plan for `git-vendored`

## Problem

The `.vendored/configs/git-semver.json` configuration may be missing entries for user-facing shipped files — command MDs, mode files, doc files, and other artifacts that should trigger a version bump when changed. Missing entries mean changes to these files don't bump the version, leading to stale version numbers that don't reflect actual changes.

## In scope for this repo

Audit `.vendored/configs/git-semver.json` in this repo:

1. Identify all user-facing shipped files: command MDs, mode files, doc files, and any other files that end users or downstream consumers interact with
2. Compare against the current `files` entries in git-semver.json
3. Add any missing file patterns that should trigger version bumps
4. Remove any stale patterns that reference files no longer present

## Reference context (handled elsewhere)

- The same audit is being performed independently in all 6 repos (pearls, madreperla, medici, git-vendored, git-semver, git-dogfood) under this epic. Each repo's audit is self-contained.

## Acceptance criteria

1. All user-facing shipped files are covered by git-semver.json file patterns
2. No stale patterns referencing non-existent files
3. `git-semver check` passes with the updated config

## Out of scope

- Changing git-semver's behavior or configuration schema
- Adding new file types not currently shipped

## Risk notes

- Overly broad glob patterns could trigger unnecessary version bumps on non-user-facing changes. Prefer specific patterns over wildcards where practical.
